name: Hide-and-Seek Mansion
desc: Multi-room classic mansion with library bookshelves, tiled bathroom, kitchen, and a grand ballroom. Beginner-friendly: the most documented indoor spots in MECCHA CHAMELEON. Paint warm wood tones and gold to match the gilded frames.

name: Indoor Country
desc: Warm-toned indoor farmhouse with cows, hay bales, red barn doors, pumpkins, and cartoon sky murals. Beginner-friendly: large color blocks reward big-body paint over fine detail. The community has charted about 6 reliable spots.

name: Sewer
desc: Dark industrial sewers with overhead pipes, red oil drums, and graffiti-covered walls. Dim lighting helps hiders because Seekers have no flashlights. The community has charted about 5 strong spots, dominated by the graffiti wall trick.

name: Backrooms
desc: Brightly lit yellow office-space homage to the Backrooms internet legend. Hardest map for hiders: intense uniform lighting leaves almost no shadows, and cover is scarce. The few save-zones are exit signs, bike-on-wall, chair stacks, and ceiling corners.

name: Penguin Hotel
desc: Fifth official map added in v1.2.0 (June 13, 2026). A winter-themed penguin hotel with lobby tiling, bedrooms, bathrooms, penguin statues, ducks, and balloons. Heavy clutter means many props to mimic but also many props for Seekers to check.

name: Osaka
desc: Sixth official map added in v1.7.0 (June 22, 2026). A Japan-themed city block with a large truck, octopus signs and statues, trash bags, plywood stacks, and open shopfronts. Smallest official map: plan tight hiding routes and use the elevated signs.
